item.description:
ChainLink 0.3.613

Guided Bike Fit wizards, smarter coaching, an easier way to get your rides in, and polish.

Getting your rides in — easier

  • First-run setup now lets you sign in to Garmin Connect right there.
  • The Import screen is reorganised by what works best: connect a service once (Garmin Connect, Ride with GPS, Karoo, Strava) at the top so your history and every new ride flow in; files and the USB cable in the middle; and the full Garmin account-export ZIP last — it's the slowest option (Garmin can take up to 30 days to email it), with a direct link to request it. Garmin Connect signs in right from that screen.

New: guided Bike Fit measurement

  • Measure step by step — wizards for your body and each bike: one measurement per screen with a diagram and plain instructions, including a proper explanation of the wall & floor method (the app does the subtraction).
  • A green-amber-red flexibility scale shows how low your bars should go, your sweet spot, and where your current bike sits — with a "guide, not a fitter" note up top.

Smarter coaching & a truer Power Profile

  • You rode — but not the plan: each planned session compares prescribed vs actual, read in context, plus a weekly roll-up. Ask the coach to adjust today's session and it rewrites it; new Critical Power test and .zwo / .fit workout import.
  • Rider type is now graded on watts-per-kilo (a light climber reads as a climber), and the Coach's Read compares your peaks against your current form instead of treating an old best as today's fitness.

Bikes, builds & fixes

  • Compare builds side by side and export a PDF; the Workshop tracks tools you've lent out.
  • Back returns you to the page you came from; tapping a ride title in Courses opens that ride; Rider Profile buttons and the expanded Courses view no longer misbehave on narrow layouts. The in-app manual has been thoroughly updated.

ChainLink 0.3.609

Guided Bike Fit wizards, smarter post-ride coaching, and a lot of polish.

New: guided Bike Fit measurement

  • Measure step by step — new wizards for your body and for each bike: one measurement per screen, with a diagram and plain instructions, so you get accurate numbers without decoding a dense form.
  • The body wizard walks inseam, sternal notch, arm, shoulders, sit-bones, foot and a flexibility test; the bike wizard levels the bike first, then talks you through saddle height, setback, saddle-to-bar and bar drop — including a proper explanation of the wall & floor method (measure to the same wall from two points; the app does the subtraction).
  • A green-amber-red flexibility scale shows how low your bars should go, your sweet spot, and where your current bike actually sits. A clear "a guide, not a substitute for a professional fit" note sits up top.

Smarter coaching around your rides

  • You rode — but not the plan: each planned session compares what was prescribed against what you actually did, read in context (your phase, recovery weeks, recent load) — so an easy day that should be easy is praised, not flagged — plus a weekly roll-up.
  • Ask the coach to adjust today's session ("I've only got 45 minutes", "make it easier") and it rewrites it; it also plans around the time you have and the days you've blocked off.
  • New Critical Power test, workout import (.zwo / .fit) as a planned session, and a fix for the coach occasionally failing silently.

Power Profile that reads the timeframe honestly

  • Your rider type is now graded on watts-per-kilo, so a lighter climber reads as a climber, not a "diesel".
  • The Coach's Read compares your career peaks against your current form (last 90 days) rather than treating an all-time best as today's fitness — and it's generated once and shared across your devices.

Bikes, builds & getting around

  • Compare builds side by side (up to three: weight, cost, part by part) and export the comparison as a PDF.
  • The Workshop tracks tools you've lent out — who has it, when, and a one-tap "returned".
  • Back now returns you to the page you came from, not the top of the Rides or Bikes list; and in Courses, tapping a ride title opens that ride.
  • The in-app manual has been thoroughly updated to cover all of this.

ChainLink 0.3.556

A new interactive ride graph, a smarter Coach, and clearer iCloud syncing.

New: explore your ride graph

  • Layer metrics together — tap the chips to overlay up to four channels at once (power, heart rate, elevation, temperature, core body temp…) and see how they move against each other.
  • Zoom in on any stretch — drag across the graph to zoom into a section; the lines refit so it fills the view. Hit Reset to zoom back out.
  • Scrub for exact numbers — hover (Mac) or drag your finger (iPhone/iPad) along the graph and a readout shows every selected metric's value at that point.
  • A linked route map — tap Show map and a route strip appears with the graph. As you scrub, a dot rides along the route so you can see where you were; zoom a graph section and the map flies to it. You can also drag the map to zoom the graph — the two stay in sync both ways.

Coach: easier to read, and it remembers

  • The Coach panel is now expandable and every reply has a Copy button, so longer answers are easy to read and reuse.
  • Conversations are saved — reopen the history to pick up a past chat, and pin the ones you want to keep. They sync across your devices.

Clearer iCloud syncing

  • A fresh install now shows an honest "Setting up ChainLink" screen with a real progress read-out instead of a wrong countdown, and your rides land as they arrive.
  • A small "Syncing with iCloud…" note now appears whenever a background sync is running for more than a moment — so an occasional slowdown always has an explanation.
  • New devices show your key numbers (fitness, totals, recent rides) almost immediately, instead of waiting for the whole library to download.
